A fast moving wildfire burned a home near the small town of Marvel.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A fast moving wildfire destroyed a home and blackened 1,250 acres of farmland and beetle-damaged forest. Wind-whipped flames reached 100 feet high. A three bedroom home near the small town of Marvel burned down shortly after it was evacuated. Additional homes in the area were voluntarily evacuated. The fire is believed to have been caused by lightning.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms produced damaging winds in Grand Junction and large hail in Silt.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong thunderstorm produced wind gusts clocked at 66 mph at a location where 24 feet of a newer wood fence got blown down. At another location about a mile away, a measured gust of 61 mph loosened roof shingles and moved a large heavy gas grill along with some heavy metal chairs.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms producing heavy rain caused flooding of areas just north of Paradox.  Normally dry arroyos were running four feet deep.  A house located two miles north of Paradox had flood waters up into the lawn.  A rockslide also occurred in the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold autumn storm system brought early season heavy snow to the mountains and some of the high valleys of western Colorado.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 4 to 7 inches occurred from Eagle to Wolcott and Edwards. 1 to 3 inches of snow fell in the Gypsum and Glenwood Springs areas. In the town of Eagle, the heavy wet snow caused several large branches to break off of trees, littering yards, roofs, and roadways. Some county owned vehicles were damaged from falling branches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold autumn storm system brought early season heavy snow to the mountains and some of the high valleys of western Colorado.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread snowfall of 5 to 8 inches occurred with wind gusts of 20 to 30   mph causing blowing and drifting snow. The combination of snow, ice, and strong winds knocked out electrical power to about 3,800 homes and businesses in Craig and Hayden for about 1.5 hours. The heavy wet snow accumulating on trees which had not lost their leaves yet resulted in numerous tree branches breaking off which contributed to the power outages.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced strong winds in Grand Junction.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorm outflow winds struck the Mantey Heights area of Grand Junction. A large potted plant and some other unsecured items were blown about by the winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms produced damaging winds in Grand Junction and large hail in Silt.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An intense thunderstorm produced very strong winds in the Mantey Heights area of Grand Junction.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused a mudslide across Highway 550 just north of Durango.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused mud and debris to flow onto Highway 550 in the 3400 block area on the north side of Durango.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain producing thunderstorms resulted in minor flooding in the Glenwood Springs and Carbondale areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced heavy rain that caused street flooding in Glenwood Springs and ponding of water on Interstate 70 near Mile Marker 114.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain producing thunderstorms resulted in minor flooding in the Glenwood Springs and Carbondale areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Runoff from heavy rain producing thunderstorms caused a small stream in Carbondale to overflow its banks. Water from the stream flowed onto a neighborhood sidewalk.  A spotter in Carbondale measured 0.60 of an inch of rain within 10 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Runoff from heavy rain producing thunderstorms caused minor flooding near Basalt.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Runoff from heavy rain caused the flooding of a road near Basalt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Runoff from heavy rain producing thunderstorms caused minor flooding and rockslides near Aspen.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Minor flooding and rockslides occurred on Highway 82 just east of Aspen.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Runoff from heavy rain producing thunderstorms caused minor flooding just sough of Carbondale.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Minor flooding was reported in the Crystal Bridge Drive area along Highway 133 just south of Carbondale.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms producing heavy rain resulted in flooding at the Black Canyon of the Gunnison National Monument and near Paradox.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain producing thunderstorms caused a flash flood which deposited mud, rocks, and debris up to 2 feet deep onto a 30 foot wide section of 175 Road at the bottom of the East Portal near Crystal Dam.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused localized flash flooding in southwest Douglas County, in the Hayman burn area.  Westcreek overflowed with a half foot of running water, over Westcreek Road on the south side of the town of Westcreek.
A line of strong to severe thunderstorms swept across much of the Tri-state area producing numerous reports of strong wind and marginally severe hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: CoCoRaHS report of broken trees.  Newspaper account nearby also mentioned blowing dirt and low visibility just north of Cheyenne Wells about this time.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Improvement in the drought conditions occurred across western Colorado as a series of Pacific storm systems brought substantial rainfall to the area. Drought conditions receded across the area leaving abnormally dry to moderate drought confined to the far northwest portion of Colorado by the end of the month.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Significant precipitation occurred across the area due to a number of autumn storm systems. As a result, the abnormally dry conditions which had been persisting across the area diminished.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong winds developed in the Front Range Foothills and portions of the Northeast Plains.  In Estes Park, the wind kicked up some gravel and debris at a playground construction site near the elementary and intermediate schools.  The debris knocked out the windows of several cars in the schools' parking lots.  Peak wind gusts generally ranged from 60 to 78 mph.  Peak wind gusts included:  78 mph at Georgetown, 70 mph at Estes Park, 62 mph; 3 miles east of Amherst, and 61 mph; 3 miles northeast of Wiggins.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong gusty winds and heavy rain buffeted Craig causing power outages throughout town and minor street flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Penny size hail occurred south of Springfield.
A thunderstorm produced marginally severe winds near Cheraw in Otero County.
Strong to high gradient winds occurred over the eastern mountains and plains.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Runoff from heavy rain producing thunderstorms caused mud and debris to flow across Highway 64 in 4 different locations 10 to 12 miles west of Meeker. Mud, rocks, trees, and other vegetation were deposited on the roadway, up to 4 feet deep in places. Some of the rocks were 3 to 4 feet in diameter. Many culverts along the highway became plugged with debris. Two semi trucks became stuck in the debris.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail from 3/4 inch to 1 inch diameter occurred. The hail broke windows on some homes. The hail came down with such force in one area that it punched holes in a 5 gallon plastic bucket.

Lightning struck a 33-ft marble statue of Jesus at Mother Cabrini Shrine, in the foothills of Golden.  The blast broke off one of the statue's arms and a hand, and also damaged a foot.  The statue was sent from Italy in five stackable sections in 1954.

Improvement in the drought conditions occurred across western Colorado as a series of Pacific storm systems brought substantial rainfall to the area. Drought conditions receded across the area leaving abnormally dry to moderate drought confined to the far northwest portion of Colorado by the end of the month.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Abnormally dry to moderate drought continued across the area. Improvement did occur as the moderate drought area receded to extreme northwest Moffat County.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Improvement in the drought conditions occurred across western Colorado as a series of Pacific storm systems brought substantial rainfall to the area. Drought conditions receded across the area leaving abnormally dry to moderate drought confined to the far northwest portion of Colorado by the end of the month.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Abnormally dry conditions plagued much of the area through the month. However, improvement did occur as moderate drought diminished across the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong winds buffeted the Interstate 70 corridor from DeBeque to New Castle as a cold autumn storm system moved into the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts of 49 to 57 mph buffeted the area.  A locally stronger gust to 59 mph was clocked at the Garfield County Airport near Rifle where some tree branches and limbs were broken off.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed along a slow-moving cold front in eastern Colorado.  Eleven tornadoes occurred in eastern Colorado, as well as strong outflow winds.  Most tornadoes remained over rural areas.  Isolated severe wind and hail reports also occurred in southwest Nebraska and northwest Kansas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Tornado occured near roads EE, 13 and occurred over open fields...no known damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms across the Northeast Plains produced large hail up to 2 inches in diameter.
A section of fence was blown into a house by severe wind gusts.

On June 26th, numerous severe thunderstorms occurred from the I-25 corridor to the far southeast plains, producing hail up to the size of baseballs, thunderstom wind gusts over 70 mph, a tornado, and flash flooding.
An upper level disturbance moved across western Colorado resulting in thunderstorm development across the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rainfall of .56 inches fell in 5 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused minor flooding in Durango.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced heavy rain and pea size hail in Durango. Minor flooding occurred across Highway 160 and 550 near the Home Depot.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed over the southeast plains and a few became severe.
A severe thunderstorm produced large hail, up to the size of quarters.

Severe thunderstorms generated hail up to 2 inches in diameter and wind gusts up to around 80 mph...which caused significant damage over portions of the city of Pueblo as well as the north side of Colorado Springs.  Well over 5,000 houses and 11,000 vehicles sustained damage in El Paso and Pueblo Counties. Damage estimates approached 100 million dollars.
Severe thunderstorms occurred in the afternoon and evening hours over parts of southeastern Colorado...bringing marginally severe hail and gusty winds to 70 mph.
An upper level trough approaching from west generated strong to severe thunderstorms during the evening hours across northeast Colorado and western Kansas.

Severe thunderstorms occurred from the I-25 corridor to the far southeastern plains.  The most severe storms occurred in northern Prowers County...where large hail...damaging wind...and tornados were reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A brief tornado was spotted.  There was no known damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced a few reports of strong outflow winds accompanied by blowing dirt.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Blowing dirt resulting from thunderstorm outflow reduced visibility to between zero and 1/4 mile at times near Yuma and Wray, Colorado.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: On June 19th, a tornado and large hail occurred near and in Pritchett.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm developed along a boundary west of Pritchett.  It spawned a tornado, which did some minor roof damage to an uninhabited dwelling.  Flying debris injured a horse.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms produced very heavy rain, large hail; up to golfball size, and damaging winds across parts of the Urban Corridor and Northeast Plains.  Flooding and flash flooding was also reported.

A major Spring storm system affecting the Central High Plains produced 16 tornadoes and flash flooding, in addition to severe hail and wind.  This was the most tornadoes reported in a single day in the Goodland County Warning Area.  The most significant tornado was an EF2 which began in Sherman county and moved north across most of Cheyenne county in Kansas.  Four homesteads suffered significant property damage along the 36 mile path length.  In addition to the EF2 tornado, there were three EF1 and twelve EF0 tornadoes stretching from Greeley county Kansas into Dundy county Nebraska.  No injuries or fatalities occurred.
Severe thunderstorms produced large hail, up to golfball size, over portions of the Northeast Plains.

On June 26th, numerous severe thunderstorms occurred from the I-25 corridor to the far southeast plains, producing hail up to the size of baseballs, thunderstom wind gusts over 70 mph, a tornado, and flash flooding.
During the late afternoon and evening hours, severe thunderstorms produced large hail and wind damage in eastern Colorado, and hail, tornadoes and flash flooding in northwestern Kansas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds flipped over irrigation pivots.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding in the Fort Collins and Wellington areas.  Nearly 5 inches of rain fell in Fort Collins, which triggered flash flooding along Fossil Creek.  Extensive street flooding was western and southern portions of the city, along with scattered power outages.  Six to eight residences were flooded in the Park Lane Mobile Home Park, off Interstate 25 and State Highway 14.  Thirty of the 60 homes in the mobile home park were affected, forcing the evacuation of several families.  Areas of the park were inundated with up to two feet of water.  ||Major flooding was also reported at the Colorado State University Campus.  Officials at C.S.U. reported minor flood damage to 18 buildings, ranging from wet carpet and ceiling tiles to soaked walls.  Minor flood damage was also reported at Fort Collins City Hall, an old police station, an administrative building and a local television state.  Up to a dozen residences had their basements flooded.  Several cars were stalled in flooded streets and intersections.  In Wellington, a trained spotter reported standing water up to waist deep in spots.
An outbreak of heavy rain producing thunderstorms resulted in flash flooding and debris flows in northwest and west central Colorado.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced .90 inches of rain in 45 minutes in downtown Oak Creek.
